title: Your AI database agent does not know what revenue means
summary: AI database agents often produce incorrect results for business questions because they lack proper metric definitions and context about how revenue is calculated. Rather than exposing raw tables, organizations should define important metrics as infrastructure with approved views and semantic context before relying on agents to answer business questions.
content:
The fastest way to get a wrong answer from an AI database agent is to ask a simple business question.
What was revenue last month?
That sounds easy.
The database has invoices, subscriptions, payments, refunds, credits, discounts, taxes, trials, failed charges, and test accounts.
The model sees tables.
Your business sees definitions.
If those definitions are not part of the system, the model has to guess.
A table called payments may include failed attempts.
subscriptions may include trials.
amount may be gross, net, pre-tax, post-tax, or stored in cents.
created_at may mean invoice creation, payment capture, or customer signup.
An AI agent can write syntactically valid SQL against all of that and still answer the wrong question.
This is why natural-language SQL needs metric context, not just schema context.
A prompt can tell the model how to calculate MRR.
An approved view makes the definition executable.
Instead of exposing raw invoice and payment tables, expose something like:
reporting.monthly_recurring_revenue

with reviewed columns, tenant scope, time grain, currency assumptions, and test-account filtering already handled.
The model still helps users ask flexible questions.
But the business definition lives in infrastructure, not in a fragile instruction.
For AI reporting, the MCP tool should carry context such as:
metric description
allowed dimensions
time zone and grain
exclusions
freshness timestamp
exact vs estimated status
scope and tenant boundaries
warnings the final answer must preserve
Otherwise the model may produce a confident answer while hiding the caveats that matter.
Longer version: Metric definitions for AI database agents
The practical rule:
If a metric is important enough for a leadership meeting, it is important enough to define before an agent calculates it.

zh.title: 你的AI数据库智能体不知道什么是收入
zh.summary: AI数据库智能体经常对业务问题给出不正确的结果，因为它们缺乏对指标的正确定义以及对收入计算方式的上下文理解。组织应该在依赖智能体回答业务问题之前，将重要指标定义为基础设施，并配备已批准的视图和语义上下文，而不是直接暴露原始表。

title: Preload vs Prefetch vs Preconnect: When to Use Each Resource Hint
summary: Developers often confuse three resource hints—preload, prefetch, and preconnect—which have different priorities and timing. Misusing them can degrade performance by putting low-priority resources in the high-priority queue and competing with critical assets like CSS and JavaScript.
content: Related: Critical CSS: What Render-Blocking Really Means and How Inlining Fixes It covers the related optimization of eliminating render-blocking CSS, which pairs well with resource hint strategy. The three resource hints (preload, prefetch, preconnect) look similar enough that developers regularly use them interchangeably. They are not interchangeable. Each one tells the browser to do a different thing with a different priority and a different timing. Using preload when you meant prefetch puts a resource in the high-priority queue and competes with CSS and critical JavaScript, potentially making the current page slower while trying to speed up a future page. Getting this wrong is worse than not using resource hints at all. What this covers: What each hint actually tells the browser, when each one fires in the page lifecycle, the browser's priority system and how hints fit into it, and concrete examples of when to use each. preload tells the browser to fetch a specific resource at high priority because the current page will need it soon. It is a declaration of intent for the current page, not a future one. The as attribute is required and tells the browser what type of resource is being preloaded. This is critical because different resource types have different priorities and different caching behaviors. Without as, the browser fetches the resource but cannot apply the correct priority, content security policy, or caching headers. Resource type as value JavaScript file script CSS stylesheet

zh.title: 预加载vs预取vs预连接：何时使用各种资源提示
zh.summary: 开发人员经常混淆三个资源提示——预加载、预取和预连接——它们具有不同的优先级和执行时序。误用这些提示可能会降低性能，因为这会将低优先级的资源放在高优先级队列中，从而与CSS和JavaScript等关键资源竞争。

title: What’s New in PHP 8.5
summary: PHP 8.5 introduces a pipe operator enabling left-to-right function composition and a built-in URI extension for structured URL handling. These features improve code readability for data transformation and provide modern alternatives to parse_url() for web applications and APIs.

zh.title: PHP 8.5新增功能
zh.summary: PHP 8.5引入了一个管道运算符，支持从左到右的函数组合，以及一个用于结构化URL处理的内置URI扩展。这些功能提高了数据转换代码的可读性，并为Web应用程序和API提供了parse_url()的现代替代方案。

title: My App Color Picker - Pixel color has Updated and Better than ever
summary: A color picker app evolved from simple pixel extraction to a comprehensive design workflow platform with semantic palettes, harmony analysis, and AI-powered inspiration. The redesign focuses on solving designers' real pain point: building consistent color systems across multiple assets rather than just finding colors.
content:
I turned my old Color Picker app into a Color Intelligence Platform for Designers


A few years ago, my app was just a simple pixel color picker.
Pick a pixel → get HEX/RGB.
Useful, but honestly… replaceable.
So I decided to completely rethink the product.
Instead of building “another color picker”, I started building a color workflow system for creators, developers, and modern brands.
Today, the new update is finally live:
Color Picker - Pixel Color on the App Store
Most color apps stop at extraction.
Mine now tries to understand color context:
Brand semantic palettes
Color harmony systems
Shade generation
Contrast analysis
Gradient systems
Wallpaper generation from color prompts
Palette extraction from photos
AI-powered color inspiration
The goal is simple:
Generate once → apply everywhere.
A single palette should work across:
UI
branding
wallpapers
presentations
social media
product mockups
One feature I’m especially excited about is Color Attention.
Instead of only extracting colors, the app analyzes:
which areas attract visual attention
dominant object colors
text visibility
color balance
focus distribution
Kind of like a heatmap for design attention.
This became surprisingly useful for:
poster design
thumbnails
social creatives
UI hierarchy checking
I noticed something interesting:
Designers don’t actually struggle with finding colors.
They struggle with:
consistency
palette scaling
semantic usage
applying colors across assets
So the app evolved from:
into:
“build a usable color system”.
The new version is optimized for:
modern iOS
high-resolution image workflows
fast palette processing
smoother UI interactions
creator-focused workflows
I also redesigned many interactions to feel less like a utility app and more like a creative tool.
Currently exploring:

live camera color tracking
LUT-style color analysis
design semantic tokens
Figma workflow integrations
brand identity generation
AI color prompts
Would love feedback from designers, developers, and indie make

zh.title: 我的应用颜色选择器 - 像素颜色已更新且比以往更好
zh.summary: 一个颜色选择器应用从简单的像素提取发展成为综合设计工作流平台，具有语义调色板、和谐分析和AI驱动的灵感。该重新设计的重点是解决设计师真实的痛点：在多个资产中构建一致的颜色系统，而不仅仅是寻找颜色。

title: AI Automation for Small Business: Why Most Projects Die Before Launch
summary: Small business AI automation projects typically fail due to integration challenges with legacy systems, API rate limits, and platform constraints—not insufficient AI capability. Success requires building resilient infrastructure with retry logic, caching, fallback protocols, and manual override interfaces.
content:
Originally published on AIdeazz — cross-posted here with canonical link.
I've built AI systems for Oracle, shipped production agents to thousands of users, and now I'm watching most small businesses burn money on automation projects that never make it past PowerPoint. The gap between what consultants promise and what actually works in production is measured in tears and invoices.
Your automation project will die at integration number three. Not because the AI isn't smart enough, but because your QuickBooks API rate limits kick in while your Shopify webhook times out and your Google Sheets connection randomly decides Tuesday afternoons are optional.
I learned this shipping a multi-agent system for a logistics company in Panama. Beautiful architecture on paper: agents for order processing, inventory management, customer support. Reality hit when their legacy ERP spoke SOAP, their warehouse system required VPN access, and their email server blocked anything that looked remotely automated.
The solution wasn't better AI—it was building a translation layer that could handle:
Retry logic with exponential backoff for every single API
Local caching to work around rate limits
Fallback protocols when primary integrations fail
Manual override interfaces for when everything breaks
Most consultants show you diagrams with clean arrows between systems. Production looks like a medieval fortress with drawbridges that only open on specific moon phases.
Everyone wants a WhatsApp bot until Meta's anti-spam systems nuke their business number. I've built Telegram and WhatsApp agents that handle thousands of daily conversations without getting banned. The difference between success and a dead number comes down to understanding platform constraints.
WhatsApp Business API isn't just expensive ($0.0085+ per message)—it requires:
Pre-approved message templates for anything outside 24-hour windows
Human handoff protocols that actually work
Rate limiting that respects both API and anti-spam thre

zh.title: 小型企业的人工智能自动化：为什么大多数项目在启动前就失败
zh.summary: 小型企业的人工智能自动化项目通常因与遗留系统的集成挑战、API速率限制和平台约束而失败——而不是人工智能能力不足。成功需要构建具有重试逻辑、缓存、回退协议和手动覆盖界面的弹性基础设施。

title: تشريح بنية الفيديو في Reddit: كيف قمنا ببناء أداة تحميل عالية الأداء باستخدام DASH وWebAssembly؟
summary: Building a high-performance Reddit video downloader requires handling DASH protocol segmentation and using FFmpeg.wasm to offload browser processing. A proxy layer handles CORS restrictions, and async promise pools enable parallel segment downloads while keeping server memory usage minimal.
content:
تشريح بنية الفيديو في Reddit: كيف قمنا ببناء أداة تحميل عالية الأداء باستخدام DASH وWebAssembly؟
twittervideodownloaderx.com
          

        
      
إذا حاولت يوماً فحص حركة مرور الشبكة (Network Tab) أثناء تشغيل فيديو على Reddit (عبر النطاق v.redd.it)، ستلاحظ شيئاً غريباً: لا يوجد ملف فيديو واحد. بدلاً من ذلك، يستخدم Reddit بروتوكول MPEG-DASH.
لأتمتة عملية التحميل، يجب على محركنا أولاً تحديد "مصدر الحقيقة" أو ملفات التعريف (Manifest Files).

المنهج التقليدي هو إرسال الروابط إلى خادم مركزي، دمجها باستخدام FFmpeg، ثم تقديمها للمستخدم. هذا النهج مكلف وبطيء.
https://twittervideodownloaderx.com/reddit_downloader_ar، نقلنا العبء الثقيل إلى متصفح المستخدم باستخدام FFmpeg.wasm.
تمنع سياسات أمان المتصفح (SOP) أي سكريبت من جلب بيانات ثنائية (Binary Data) مباشرة من نطاقات Reddit بسبب قيود CORS.
 يقوم العميل بإرسال روابط الأجزاء (Segments) إلى البروكسي الخاص بنا.
 يقوم البروكسي بإزالة رؤوس CORS المقيدة من Reddit.
 يضيف البروكسي رأس Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *.
 يتم تمرير البيانات كـ ReadableStream إلى العميل. هذا النهج يضمن بقاء استهلاك الذاكرة في خادمنا ثابتاً بغض النظر عن حجم الفيديو.
تتكون فيديوهات DASH من مئات الأجزاء الصغيرة. تحميلها بشكل متسلسل يمثل عنق زجاجة. قمنا بتنفيذ تجمع وعود غير متزامن (Async Promise Pool):
بناء أداة تحميل لـ Reddit ليس مجرد "كشط" روابط. إنه تمرين في هندسة الويب الحديثة يوازن بين البروكسي في جانب الخادم ومعالجة WebAssembly في جانب العميل.
twittervideodownloaderx.com
          

        
      
الوسوم: #JavaScript #WebDev #NodeJS #WebAssembly #FFmpeg #Reddit #Streaming #Architecture

zh.title: Reddit视频结构解析：我们如何使用DASH和WebAssembly构建高性能下载工具
zh.summary: 构建高性能Reddit视频下载器需要处理DASH协议分割和使用FFmpeg.wasm来卸载浏览器处理。代理层处理CORS限制，异步承诺池使并行段下载成为可能，同时将服务器内存使用量保持在最低限度。

title: 我把三个3D打印工具整合成了一个——CLI和Web都能用
summary: Three independent 3D printing tools were unified with optional soft dependencies and lazy imports, maintaining repository separation while enabling integrated workflows through CLI flags and web UI panels. The approach eliminates user friction without creating hard coupling between projects.
content:
背景


我之前写了三个独立的3D打印工具，各有定位：
SupportSage — AI 优化的支撑结构生成（树状支撑 + 柱状支撑）
FilamentDB — 开源耗材参数数据库，查 nozzle/bed 温度等
Printsight — 打印件照片质量检测，识别拉丝/层纹/翘曲
三个工具各自能干活。但用户得记三条命令、三个安装方式、三个独立的输出格式。割裂感很明显。
有人问了一句：能不能整合？Web 版也能用吗？
我的原则是保持三个仓库独立，不在 SupportSage 里加硬依赖。但提供两个旗标做"软连接"：
# 分析 + 自动显示 Bambu Lab PLA Basic 的推荐参数
supportsage analyze model.stl --filament "Bambu Lab PLA Basic"

# 生成支撑 + 耗材上下文
supportsage tree model.stl -o optimized.stl --filament "eSun PLA+"

输出变成：
🌳 Tree Support Generator
  Strategy: balanced

  🌟 Supports generated: optimized.stl

  💡 Filament: Bambu Lab PLA Basic
     🔥 Nozzle: 220°C (range 200-230°C)
     🔥 Bed:    55°C (range 50-60°C)
     ⚡ Max volumetric: 12 mm³/s
     ↩  Retraction: 0.8mm @ 30mm/s
     🌬️ Fan: 100%
     🛏️ Adhesion: brim

# 生成支撑 → 查耗材参数 → 检测打印质量
supportsage tree model.stl -o out.stl --filament "PLA+" --inspect after_print.jpg

一条命令跑完：支撑生成 → 参数推荐 → 质量检测。
Web 版（supportsage-gui --open→浏览器）加了三个新面板：
STL 上传 + 分析/支撑生成（原有）
耗材搜索面板 — 输入名字搜索 FilamentDB，看到匹配结果和参数
打印件检测面板 — 上传打印照片，返回质量评分和缺陷详情
API 端点也新增了 /api/filament-search、/api/filament-recommend、/api/print-inspect，方便其他工具调用。
这是最关键的决策。FilamentDB 和 Printsight 都是可选的——用户只装了 SupportSage 也能正常工作：
def _print_filament_info(filament_arg):
    try:
        from filamentdb.database import recommend
    except ImportError:
        print("  ⚠️ filamentdb not installed — skipping filament lookup")
        return
    # ... 查参数并打印

不破坏现有工作流，新功能加上了就是 bonus。
Web GUI 用的是 Python 内置的 http.server，没有 Flask/Django。新增端点同样用 lazy import 处理缺失依赖：
def _handle_filament_search(self, data):
    try:
        from filamentdb.database import search
        results = search(data.get("query", ""))
        self._send_json({"results": results})
    except ImportError:
        self._send_json({"error": "FilamentDB not installed"}, 500)

前端纯 vanilla JS + CSS（32KB 单页），不需要 npm 或构建工具。
之前用户要分别运行三条 pip install 命令，还要知道每个包的 wheel URL。现在：
bash <(curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bossman-lab/supportsage/main/scripts/install_3d_tools.sh)

一

zh.title: 我把三个3D打印工具整合成了一个——CLI和Web都能用
zh.summary: 三个独立的3D打印工具通过可选软依赖和延迟导入统一在一起，在保持库分离的同时，通过CLI标志和Web UI面板实现集成工作流。这种方法消除了用户摩擦，避免了项目之间的紧耦合。

title: Разбор архитектуры Reddit: Как создать высокопроизводительный загрузчик видео с поддержкой DASH и HLS
summary: Reddit video downloaders must handle DASH/HLS streaming protocols with segmented content and manage CORS restrictions through a proxy layer. Processing is offloaded to the browser via FFmpeg.wasm with async promise pools for efficient parallel downloads of hundreds of small video segments.
content:
Для обычного пользователя загрузка видео из интернета кажется тривиальной задачей: найти ссылку на .mp4 и сохранить файл. Однако для разработчиков, работающих с такими гигантами, как Reddit, реальность оказывается гораздо сложнее.
twittervideodownloaderx.com
          

        
      
Если вы попробуете проанализировать сетевой трафик видео на Reddit через DevTools, вы заметите, что единого видеофайла не существует. Вместо этого браузер загружает сотни мелких сегментов (.m4s или .ts).
Первым шагом к автоматизации загрузки является поиск «источника истины» — файлов манифеста (.mpd для DASH или .m3u8 для HLS).

Традиционные загрузчики отправляют потоки на сервер, объединяют их с помощью FFmpeg и передают готовый файл пользователю. Это неэффективно и дорого с точки зрения инфраструктуры.
https://twittervideodownloaderx.com/reddit_downloader_ru мы перенесли тяжелую обработку в браузер пользователя с помощью FFmpeg.wasm.
Политика безопасности браузера запрещает скрипту на одном домене напрямую скачивать бинарные данные с домена v.redd.it.
 Клиент отправляет запросы на сегменты видео/аудио через наш прокси.
 Прокси удаляет ограничивающие CORS-заголовки CDN Reddit.
 Прокси добавляет заголовок Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *.
 Данные передаются обратно клиенту через ReadableStream. Такой подход гарантирует, что использование оперативной памяти нашим сервером остается минимальным, независимо от размера видео.
Видео в форматах DASH/HLS состоят из сотен фрагментов. Последовательная загрузка каждого фрагмента создает узкое место. Мы внедрили асинхронный пул обещаний (Promise Pool):
Создание загрузчика для Reddit — это не просто написание скрипта для парсинга ссылок. Это упражнение в современной веб-архитектуре, требующее баланса между проксированием на стороне сервера и обработкой данных на стороне клиента с помощью WebAssembly.
Reddit Video Downloader (Русская версия)
Tags: #JavaScript #WebDev #NodeJS #WebAssembly #FFmpeg #Reddit #Streaming #Architecture

zh.title: Reddit架构解析：如何创建支持DASH和HLS的高性能视频下载器
zh.summary: Reddit视频下载器必须处理DASH/HLS流媒体协议和分段内容，并通过代理层管理CORS限制。处理通过FFmpeg.wasm卸载到浏览器中，使用异步promise池高效并行下载数百个小视频片段。

title: From Pixels to Calories: Mastering Precise Food Estimation with Vision AI
summary: A high-precision food volume estimation system combines Segment Anything Model for pixel-perfect food isolation with GPT-4o for contextual analysis, enabling transformation of photos into detailed nutritional breakdowns. The pipeline bridges computer vision precision with large language model reasoning.
content:
We’ve all been there: staring at a delicious plate of Carbonara, trying to log it into a fitness app, only to realize the "standard serving" is wildly different from what’s actually on the plate. Most Vision Multimodal apps fail because they can identify the what (it's pasta!) but fail at the how much (is it 200g or 500g?). 
In this guide, we are bridging that gap by building a high-precision food volume estimation engine. By leveraging the Segment Anything Model (SAM) for pixel-perfect object isolation and the GPT-4o API for contextual reasoning, we can transform a simple smartphone photo into a detailed nutritional breakdown. Whether you're building a health app or exploring Computer Vision workflows, this "Learning in Public" project will level up your AI engineering game. 
The secret sauce isn't just one model; it’s a pipeline. We use OpenCV for preprocessing, SAM to "carve out" the food from the plate, and GPT-4o to act as the "Digital Nutritionist" who understands depth and density.
graph TD
    A[User Uploads Image] --> B[OpenCV: Resize & Pre-process]
    B --> C[Segment Anything: Generate Masks]
    C --> D[Identify Food vs. Reference Objects]
    D --> E[GPT-4o Vision: Analyze Volume & Context]
    E --> F[Pydantic Validation: Structured JSON]
    F --> G[FastAPI Response: Calories & Macros]

Before we dive into the code, ensure you have the following in your tech_stack:
Python 3.10+
Segment Anything (SAM) weights (sam_vit_h_4b8939.pth)
OpenAI API Key (specifically for the GPT-4o model)
FastAPI for the backend
Traditional bounding boxes are too "noisy." SAM allows us to generate precise masks that calculate the exact pixel area of the food. This is crucial for determining volume relative to a reference object (like a fork or the plate size).
import numpy as np
import torch
import cv2
from segment_anything import sam_model_registry, SamPredictor

# Initialize SAM
sam_checkpoint = "sam_vit_h_4b8939.pth"
model_type = "vit_h"
device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_av

zh.title: 从像素到卡路里：用Vision AI掌握精确的食物估算
zh.summary: 一个高精度的食物体积估算系统将Segment Anything Model用于像素完美的食物隔离，与GPT-4o的上下文分析相结合，能够将照片转化为详细的营养成分分解。该管道桥接了计算机视觉的精度和大型语言模型的推理能力。

title: A 350-Line GLSL Shader Playground in the Browser — WebGL Init, Line-Number-Aware Errors, and URL-Hash Sharing
summary: A minimal shader playground (~350 lines of vanilla JavaScript) enables live fragment shader editing with real-time compilation, line-number-aware error reporting, and URL-hash-based code sharing without build tools. The implementation demonstrates practical WebGL context setup and debounced compilation.
content:
Sometimes you want to try a shader without spinning up Shadertoy, without a build step, without anything. Just a textarea, a canvas, and a refresh-while-you-type loop. This is that page: ~350 lines of vanilla JS, a fixed full-screen-quad vertex shader, and a fragment shader you edit live. Compile errors land back in the UI with line numbers; the URL hash round-trips your source so links shared on Twitter just work.

🌐 Demo: https://sen.ltd/portfolio/shader-playground/
GitHub: https://github.com/sen-ltd/shader-playground
The vertex shader is fixed — a full-screen quad as two triangles.
The fragment shader is what the user edits. The page runs gl.compileShader(...) 220 ms after each keystroke (debounced) and swaps the program if compilation succeeds.
Three uniforms are auto-bound: u_resolution (drawing buffer size), u_mouse (cursor in WebGL pixel coords), u_time (seconds since page load).
Compile errors land in the UI with line numbers, parsed from the driver's info log.
The shader source round-trips through location.hash as base64url, so sharing a permalink works the same in any browser.
That's it. The rest of this post is the small list of footguns the implementation hits.
const gl =
  els.canvas.getContext("webgl", {
    antialias: true,
    premultipliedAlpha: false,
  }) ||
  els.canvas.getContext("experimental-webgl");

Two flags that almost always come up:
premultipliedAlpha: false is the difference between "canvas is an opaque drawing surface" and "canvas blends into the DOM behind it". If you forget this and your fragment shader writes gl_FragColor = vec4(rgb, 1.0), you'll still see the page background bleeding through on some browsers — and you'll spend ten minutes wondering why. Setting it explicitly removes the surprise.
antialias: true is the default, but make it explicit so you remember it's there. MSAA on the swapchain makes shape edges look clean without any per-fragment MSAA logic.
The experimental-webgl fallback is mostly historical at this point (A

zh.title: 浏览器中的 350 行 GLSL 着色器游乐场 — WebGL 初始化、行号感知错误和 URL 哈希共享
zh.summary: 一个最小的着色器游乐场(约 350 行纯 JavaScript)能够进行实时片段着色器编辑、实时编译、行号感知错误报告以及基于 URL 哈希的代码共享，无需构建工具。该实现演示了实用的 WebGL 上下文设置和防抖编译。

title: How to Migrate a Production Stack to a New Region Without Downtime
summary: Regional migration failures typically result from multiple simultaneous issues: DNS caching past TTLs, in-flight write loss, session breaks, async job interruption, and stale infrastructure records. Solutions require maintaining single authoritative sources during cutover and comprehensive state management across all system components.
content:
Last spring I moved an entire production stack — database, object storage, app servers, mail — from one cloud region to another. Different continent, different provider, same users. The plan looked clean on paper. The execution? Less clean.
If you've ever tried to physically relocate a running system, you know the feeling. Stuff that worked locally suddenly doesn't. DNS lies to you. Half your users hit the old server, half hit the new one, and the database that was supposed to be "caught up" is mysteriously 12 minutes behind.
This post is about why naive cutovers fail and how to do one that actually works.
I've seen this approach proposed in a dozen migration docs. Spin up the new stack, dump and restore the database, change the DNS record, done. It almost never works that cleanly, and the reason is rarely a single thing — it's a pile of small things that all hit at once.
Here's what actually goes wrong:
DNS TTLs aren't honored. Some resolvers cache aggressively past your TTL. You'll have traffic hitting the old IP for hours, sometimes days.
In-flight writes get lost. Anything written to the old database between your snapshot and the cutover is gone unless you've planned for it.
Stateful sessions break. Users logged into the old box get bounced to the new one with no session.
Async jobs disappear. Queue workers on the old machine pick up jobs and then get killed mid-execution.
Email auth breaks silently. Your new IP isn't in the SPF record, so deliverability tanks for 48 hours before anyone notices.
Individually, each of these is fixable. Together, on cutover day, they form a small disaster.
The core issue is that during cutover, you briefly run two copies of your system that both think they're authoritative. Writes can land on either. Reads can come from either. Whichever side has "the truth" depends on which resolver a client used and when.
The fix is to never have two writable sources of truth at the same time. You either:
Keep the old side authoritative and repl

zh.title: 如何在不停机的情况下将生产堆栈迁移到新区域
zh.summary: 区域迁移失败通常由多个同时发生的问题引起：DNS 缓存超过 TTL、写入丢失、会话中断、异步作业中断和过时的基础设施记录。解决方案需要在切换期间维护单一的权威来源，并在所有系统组件中进行全面的状态管理。

title: Foreboding AI, One Year Later: What Are We Really Building?
summary: Beyond concerns about AI replacing humans, the focus now shifts to the consequences of autonomous AI systems that can read files, run commands, edit code, and manage infrastructure. The critical challenge becomes establishing human oversight and institutional responsibility frameworks before deploying AI as autonomous agents.
content:
A year ago, I wrote Foreboding AI: The Inevitable Collapse We’re Funding Ourselves
At the time, my concern was that we were paying for the very systems that could eventually replace us. We were subscribing to tools that learnt from our prompts, our corrections, our habits, our work, and our impatience.
A year later, I still think that concern was valid.
But my worry has changed shape.
It is no longer only about whether AI can answer better, write better, summarise better, draw better, or code better.
The deeper question now is this:
What happens when AI stops simply answering and starts acting?
For years, most people understood AI as a chatbot.
You asked a question.
It gave an answer.


Maybe it helped write an email.


Maybe it explained a piece of code.


Maybe it generated an image.



That felt powerful, but still contained.
Now we are moving into something different.
AI systems can now:
browse websites,


read files,


inspect codebases,


run terminal commands,


call APIs,


edit code repositories,


use external tools,


remember context,


and work through multi-step tasks.



That is not just a better chatbot.
That is a system with hands.
And once AI has hands, the questions become much harder:
What can it read?


What can it change?


What can it delete?


What can it install?


What secrets can it see?


What systems can it touch?


Who is watching it?


Who is responsible when it gets something wrong?



And maybe the hardest question of all:
Are we giving AI responsibility before we have built enough human restraint around it?
One of the biggest changes I have observed over the last year is how quickly AI development tools have shifted from assistance to delegation.
Not long ago, AI coding tools were mostly autocomplete and chat-in-the-editor helpers. They could suggest a function, explain a bug, or produce a snippet.
Useful? Absolutely.
But the human still clearly owned the work.
Now we have tools that can:
read a codebase,


interpret an issue,


mak

zh.title: Foreboding AI，一年后：我们真正在构建什么？
zh.summary: 超越对人工智能取代人类的担忧，焦点现在转向自主人工智能系统的后果，这些系统可以读取文件、运行命令、编辑代码和管理基础设施。关键挑战变成在部署人工智能作为自主代理之前，建立人类监督和机构责任框架。
